---
title: "LRP78: Interventional-effects decomposition of word reading via letter sounds"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Substantially edited by a LLM-based AI tool (Codex/GPT-5).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

Every other mediation model reports **natural** direct and indirect effects (NDE/NIE). The DAG's identification analysis states that dose is a **treatment-induced mediator–outcome confounder** of the letter-sound → reading path, so the cross-world natural effects are formally unidentified. The randomised **interventional** analogues remain well-defined in that structure because the mediator is drawn from its arm-specific distribution rather than set to the same child's incompatible natural counterfactual.

LRP78 is the interventional companion to [LRP59](../lrp-rli-med-059): the same joint mediator + outcome model ($L \to W$) and the same adjustment set, but the g-formula labels the decomposition as **interventional direct and indirect effects** (IDE/IIE). It removes the recanting-witness obstacle from the target estimand; it does not remove latent general-ability confounding.

## Design

Same as LRP59 (phase 0, $n \approx 53$). The mediator is drawn from the fitted **covariate-conditional** law $P(M\mid C,g)$ within each covariate profile; it is not permuted across children. In this fully parametric model the natural and interventional branches therefore use the same numerical functional. LRP78's IIE and LRP59's NIE should agree up to posterior-sampling and g-formula Monte Carlo error. Agreement is an implementation check, not evidence that dose causes no distortion; a material gap would first indicate non-comparable fits or Monte Carlo error. The t3 temporal-ordering sensitivity is skipped because it is a natural-effect construction.

## Assumptions

The interventional effects avoid the *cross-world* problem created by the treatment-induced confounder, but the mediator–outcome relationship is **still $GA$-confounded**. Moreover, dose is not represented in the fitted model, so this calculation cannot estimate or diagnose dose distortion. IDE/IIE remain **model-based adjusted decompositions** under stated assumptions — a weaker-assumption estimand class, not an identified causal route. $n \approx 53 \Rightarrow$ wide intervals.

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_mediation.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
